title: Binary Tree Basics with Peter and Stewie

dialogue.line: Stewie, what's a binary tree? Is it like binary code?
No, Peter! Think of it like a family tree, but for data.
So, it has branches and all that?
Exactly! Each point is a 'node' with up to two 'child' nodes.
The top node's the 'root', and we split data as we go down.
Why's that useful? Got a story for it?
Sure! It helps organize and search data efficiently, like a library index.
Ah! So it's the Dewey Decimal of computers!
Precisely, if Dewey liked nesting dolls!
Got it! No more binary babble from me!
Thank goodness! Now, if only you understood fractions...
